feedback.text: I would like to highlight a few additional topics that I believe are worth considering: Advertisement and gambling targeting minors: a) Influencers often sell their own merchandise, and advertise this in their content (e.g. YouTube videos). When the audience is primarily children, this becomes problematic when there's no clear distinction between the content and the advertisements. Additionally, it seems reasonable to limit the amount of advertisement so that the majority of the running time cannot in practice be advertising various products. b) Influencers who broadcast themselves gambling online, playing slots etc., should have their content age-restricted to prevent gambling among children. c) Influencers who broadcast themselves engaging in gambling-like activities in video games and collectables, such as opening loot boxes in games or Pokémon card packs, should also have their content age-restricted for the same reason. Dark pattern: undefined terms and vague labelling: a) In video games, when the user wants to purchase in-game currency, they are generally presented with a handful of options ranging from a few euros up to a hundred. These are often labelled as "best value", "most popular", "+300 free gems" etc. to nudge the user towards a more expensive option. It seems reasonable to enforce some kind of truth in advertising here, and add something like a "cost per unit" measure like what grocery stores provide to help the user compare one product with another. Destruction of video games and other creative digital works: a) When video games are sold, it's unclear if they depend on a publisher-provided service to function, and if so, when that service may be terminated. The consumer should be informed before purchase exactly what they are buying: is it a stand-alone product that they are in control of, or is it essentially a time-limited service. For products that are sold (as opposed to subscribed to as a service), the publisher should be obligated to provide an end-of-life plan, where the necessary software and documentation is provided to the customers, allowing them to keep their copies in a functioning state. Additionally, as creative digital works become more prominent in our culture, there's also value in conserving these works in a functioning state for future generations.
